defect: none. Core claims all in source: 'The founders of Paragon include Ehud Barak... and Ehud Schneorson, the former commander of Israel's Unit 8200', 'First Look at Paragon's Proliferating Spyware Operations', 'On January 31, 2025, WhatsApp sent notifications to approximately 90 WhatsApp accounts', 'Francesco Cancellato is the Editor in Chief of Fanpage.it', migrant-rescue victims, zero-click all confirmed. Only two peripheral parenthetical details ('infections 2024-12-14' exact day, 'later corroborated by Italian prosecutors') are not in this English source text, but these are non-load-bearing color and the source states it has 'dates of infection for each individual'. Not a refuting defect.

Two issues. (1) Duplicate doctrine_links: two entries both doctrine_id 'kp/8th-congress-defense-plan', both confidence 'attested', both same attesting_source_id (jy1498) - structural redundancy. (2) Summary's companion-event cross-reference is not in the source: 'Sim Hyon Sop action of one month prior (jy1435, 24 April 2023)' - 'Sim Hyon Sop' 0 hits, 'jy1435' 0 hits, 'April 24' 0 hits in jy1498. Core designations (Pyongyang University of Automation, 110th Research Center, Chinyong, Kim Sang Man, WMD language) all verified and the doctrine goal is attested honestly.

The attributing_org string puts words in Proofpoint's mouth that the source does not say: it claims Proofpoint 'characterises TA427 as DPRK-aligned in support of the Reconnaissance General Bureau' - but 'Reconnaissance General Bureau' and 'RGB' appear 0 times in the fetched Proofpoint text. This is an attribution framed more specifically (RGB tasking) than the cited source supports. The prepass itself conceded this ('RGB framing... not in the fetched Proofpoint text'). All event facts (ClickFix, QuasarRAT, 'fewer than five' think tanks, TA450/MuddyWater, TA422/APT28, Kimsuky/Emerald Sleet) are supported.

attested doctrine link 'uk/ncs-2016' is NOT substantiated by the fetched source text, which is gov.uk publication-page nav-chrome. The reasoning attests the link to the NCS 2016 'defend' pillar, but 'defend' (0 hits), 'GCHQ' (0), 'Active Cyber Defence' (0), 'National Cyber Security Centre' (0) do not appear in the fetched text - it only confirms the strategy page title exists. The prepass itself rated event support 'partial' and called the source 'page chrome... not the defend-pillar body', yet stamped clean. An attested confidence requires the attesting source to name the strategic goal; this fetch does not. Underlying historical facts are true and well-known, but the cited source as fetched does not carry the attested doctrine.
